title: The Hidden Tax of Productivity: Solving for 'AI Brain Fry'

content_blocks.title: The Impact of Intensive AI Oversight

items.label: Increase in mental effort for high-oversight tasks
Increase in decision fatigue among affected workers
Increase in major error frequency for those with 'brain fry'
Higher intent to leave among top AI users experiencing fatigue

content_blocks.title: Emotional Burnout vs. AI Brain Fry

items.a: Driven by repetitive, unenjoyable 'toil' and routine tasks.
items.b: Driven by intense monitoring, oversight, and information overload.

items.a: Alleviated when AI replaces routine or repetitive work.
items.b: Worsened when AI increases workload or tool-toggling.

items.a: Characterized by physical and emotional exhaustion.
items.b: Characterized by 'mental static,' fog, and slower decision-making.

content_blocks.title: Leadership Strategies to Mitigate Cognitive Strain
points: Cap simultaneous tool use: Productivity gains tend to dip and cognitive load spikes after using more than three AI tools at once.
Eliminate the 'AI Orphan Tax': Providing manager support and answering AI-related questions can reduce mental fatigue by 15%.
Shift from activity to impact: Avoid rewarding token consumption or code volume, which incentivizes work intensification over quality.
Redesign for human + AI limits: Treat human attention as a finite resource and define specific 'spans of control' for agent oversight.
